fields.summary: Purchasing Tracker Filters not working

content.text: On Test, none of the filters are working in the Purchasing Tracker. Filters should filter the table view and the Export output.

fields.summary: Dont allow Duplicate Department

content.text: Jira Story: Prevent Duplicate Departments in Maintenance Admin
the system to validate that a department name does not already exist when I attempt to add a new one,
I can avoid data redundancy and maintain a clean, accurate list of departments.

Currently, the "Add" button on the Departments tab of the Maintenance Admin screen allows users to save department names that are already present in the database. This story introduces a backend and frontend check to ensure all department names are unique.
When the "Add" button is clicked, the system must perform a case-insensitive check against existing department names.
If the department name already exists, the record must not be saved.
An error message must be displayed to the user:
"Department '[Name]' already exists. Please enter a unique name."
Validation should trigger both on the front-end (immediate feedback) and the back-end (to prevent API-level duplicates).
Record saved as "Roofing" (system should trim whitespace).
has a unique constraint (optional but recommended).
The check should ignore leading and trailing whitespace.

Stop departments in the Maintnance Admin screen when on the Departments tab, at the bottom there is an add button to add a new department but it allows you to enter a department that already exists.  This should be blocked by a check to see if the one entered already exists.

fields.summary: Alternate Approvers Ability to Rectify

content.text: As a user that is setup as an alternate approver for another user, I want the ability to perform the rectify action on a time entry for employees of the other user I am set as the alternate approver for.  This is so that if they are away or unable to rectify, I can do it in their place so that their employee’s timesheets can be completed.
I am User A.  I am setup as the alternate approver for User B in User B’s user profile.
When I go to the Timesheet module and the Review Timesheets tab and look at an employee that is for User B, I should now be able to do any Rectify actions that User B was able to do.
I am User A.  I am removed from alternate approver for User B in their user profile.
When I go to the Timesheet module and the Review Timesheets tab and look at an employee that is for User B, I should now NOT be able to do any Rectify actions that User B was able to do.
Below is the original request from the user:
You want it so that if User A is an alternate approver for User B, then User A can rectify any timesheet for User B's employees right?

correct. Right now he can approve tinesheets but nothing else. Example is Justin Brogi is on PTO and Michael Millsap is the alternate approver, but Millsap cannot rectify anything. I had to enable the UAC control to allow rectify on any employee for now

fields.summary: Import Costs - dont allow multiple submissions

content.text: While working on the import costs module if one of the uploads is large than the user is able to click the submit button more than one time and it submits the form multiple times. We need to block this.

fields.summary: Owner contacted should only be required on the single action not after

content.text: The owner contacted field has a key in the keys admin that specifies which action of the work order workflow it is required at. Currently this set it up as required on that action and afterwards. As a user of the work order, I only want that key to make it required on that specific action and not after. This is because that field is only needed that one time and then it's stored in the audit.
If the key is set to complete, then that means it is required on before or after
If the key is not set at all or if the key is empty, then that field will not be required at all
the key is called ownerContactRequired in the keys admin

fields.summary: Log Cron - adjust to move logs to new location

content.text: Log files are reset each day to make new log_* folders.  However after 30 days we want the old log_* folders to be moved to a new path so they are not filling up disk space on the main production drive.  Provide a key that can be set to specify the path that the log_* folders should be moved to after 30 days.

fields.summary: Incident Admin - Filter on 'Employee Involved' Resets Unexpectedly

content.text: "Employee Involved" column filter to persist and display only the filtered results
on the New Incident Admin page, so that I can reliably and efficiently locate incident records tied to a specific individual.
where applying a filter to the

column causes the results to briefly show the correct, filtered data before the filter is automatically cleared/reset, returning the table to a complete, unfiltered state. This behavior prevents reliable searching and sorting.

It is suspected that a component re-render or asynchronous data fetch is overwriting the applied filter state. The fix must ensure that the filter state is maintained until manually cleared by the user.
When an Admin User applies any filter condition (e.g.,
column, the filter criteria must remain active and the displayed data must not revert to the unfiltered view.
The resulting table data must accurately reflect the applied filter criteria.
The filter condition and value must remain visibly present in the column filter UI until the user explicitly clears the filter.
. 2. Click on the filter icon for the
records where the Employee Involved field contains the text "Mitchell." The filter remains visibly active.
The table briefly displays the filtered results, then the filter clears out automatically, and the table shows all records again (Actual Current Behavior).
records where the Employee Involved field starts with the text "Sar" (e.g., Sarah Smith). The filter remains visibly active.

fields.summary: Incident Admin - Implement Filter Display Bar in Incident Admin AG Grid

content.text: As a system administrator or incident manager,
I want applied filters within the Incident Admin Module's AG Grid to be displayed in a visible bar at the top of the grid, matching the Purchasing Module's functionality,
So that I can easily view all active filters and manage them efficiently by removing them individually without needing to re-open the column filter menus.

This story covers implementing a persistent, visible filter display bar for the AG Grid component within the Incident Admin Module. This bar must show all currently applied column filters as individual, removable chips or tags, mirroring the existing behavior in the Purchasing Module.
The AG Grid in the Incident Admin Module must dynamically display a filter chip/tag bar when one or more filters are active.
The UI/UX (styling, positioning, chip format) of the filter bar must be consistent with the implementation in the Purchasing Module.
Each active filter must be represented by a clear, distinct chip labeled with the
Each filter chip must include a clickable element (e.g., an 'X' icon) to allow the user to remove that specific filter.
Removing a filter via the chip must immediately clear the filter for that column and update the grid data view without a full page refresh.
When all filters are removed (either individually or via column menus), the filter display bar must disappear.
User opens 'Status' column filter and selects 'Open'.
Grid updates to show only 'Open' incidents. A filter chip labeled
. User then applies 'Priority' column filter and selects 'High'.
Grid updates with both filters applied. Two chips appear:
active, user clicks the 'X' on the
chip is removed. Grid updates to show all incidents with
User clicks the 'X' on the last remaining filter chip.
The chip is removed. Grid shows all incidents (no filters applied). The filter display bar is removed from the screen.
Screenshot Example of the Purchasing Module filters

fields.summary: Project Tracker - multiple fields with same display name

content.text: The issue is that there are two project tracker custom fields that are different data elements, but they have the same display name. This typically would be not an issue because they are on different projects but now that we have the multiple project selector, when both projects are shown at once the project tracker does not know how to handle it since there's two with the same name.  Same thing with a bookmark that is for multiple projects.

fields.summary: Better handling of Purchasing tracker auto refresh

content.text: 📝 User Story: Improve Purchasing Tracker Auto Refresh Handling
I Want To: Have the system efficiently manage data refreshes
So That: Performance issues are avoided, even if multiple refreshes are triggered in quick succession.
AC1: The Purchasing Tracker must prevent multiple simultaneous or redundant data refreshes initiated by the same user.
AC2: If a user attempts to trigger multiple refreshes rapidly, only one refresh should be processed at a time; additional requests should be ignored or queued appropriately.
AC3: The system must maintain stable performance and avoid excessive load caused by repeated refresh actions from a single user.
AC4: Users should receive clear feedback if a refresh is already in progress.
Input: User clicks the refresh button 12 times in quick succession.
Output: Only one data refresh is executed; the user is notified that a refresh is already in progress if they attempt additional refreshes.

The purchasing tracker caused a performance issue on production. The same user was spawning a dozen different data refreshes consistently. We need to handle this.  There is an auto-refresh on that particular tracker and it is what is causign the problem.

fields.summary: Project Admin - Prevent Type Changes for Calculated Fields in Project Admin

content.text: 📝 User Story: Prevent Type Changes for Calculated Fields in Project Admin
I Want To: Be prevented from changing the type of calculated fields in both system and custom fields within the Project Admin interface
So That: Data integrity is maintained and calculated fields function as intended without risk of misconfiguration.
AC1: In the Project Admin interface, calculated fields (both system and custom) must have their field type selection disabled or locked.
AC2: Any attempt to change the type of a calculated field should be blocked, with a clear message explaining that type changes are not allowed for calculated fields.
AC3: Non-calculated fields should continue to allow type changes as per current functionality.
Input: Admin attempts to edit a calculated field and change its type from "Calculated" to another type.
Output: The type selection is disabled or, if attempted, an error message appears: "Type changes are not allowed for calculated fields."
Dont allow calculated fields for their types to be changed in Project Admin system or custom fields

fields.summary: Project Admin - Optimize CSV Upload in Project Admin Drivers

content.text: 📝 User Story: Optimize CSV Upload in Project Admin Drivers
I Want To: Have the "Upload" button on the /projects/admin /Drivers/index page optimized for better performance when uploading CSV files
So That: I can efficiently upload driver data without changing the current results or workflow.
AC1: The "Upload" button on the /projects/admin /Drivers/index page must be optimized to improve the speed and reliability of CSV file uploads.
AC2: The upload process must continue to produce the same results as before (no changes to data processing or output).
AC3: The "Export" button should still provide a file that can be used for updates via the optimized upload process.
AC4: No changes should be made to the user interface or workflow, except for the improved upload performance.
Input: User clicks "Export" to download a CSV, modifies the file, and uses the "Upload" button to re-upload the updated CSV.
Expected Output: The upload completes faster and more reliably than before, and the driver data is updated as expected with no change in results.
"/projects/admin /Drivers/index "Upload" button need to optimize. Use "Export" to get a file to use for update.
In the Project Admin, Upload button needs to be optimized.  Results need to stay the same. We just want to optimize the upload of the csv file."

fields.summary: Log Processing for errors and debug information

content.text: Currently in the app/tmp/logs there is an error.log and debug.log
these contain a lot of errors that are happening.  we need to create some automated analysis of these logs to determine what changes need to be made.

Currently the admins/resetlogs action is a cron job so it fires once a day.  I want to tie this process to that cron job so that when it fires it takes the old logs and gets them reviewed.  here is how I am brainstorming this could work:
The cron job grabs the 2 files (error and debug logs) if they exist
Creates a new Jira SM defect called “Logs {date}” and automatically assigns it to the developer (Darl in this case) and puts it in the To Do status

Dev could have a claude scheduled task that nightly picks up that ticket, analyzes the log files, suggests code changes for them and has the plan ready for him to review and puts the ticket then in the “In Progress” status for him
Dev reviews the plan, adjusts it or determines if it is necessary or not or if it is a larger issue that needs its own ticket.
Dev implements the fixes and commits like a normal ticket.
Over time this process would get us ideally to zero log items in those files if we are fixing things but for now since there is technical debt we need to put in the time to handle them.

fields.summary: DUMMY - Automated Testing Setup Verification

content.text: 📝 User Story: Automated QA Testing Pipeline Verification
As A: QA Engineer or Developer
I Want To: create and execute a dummy ticket in the system
So That: I can verify that the automated QA testing pipeline is functioning correctly and tickets are processed as expected.
AC1: A dummy ticket can be created in the system without errors.
AC2: The automated QA testing pipeline must detect and process the dummy ticket.
AC3: The results of the pipeline run (pass/fail) must be visible and verifiable.
AC4: The dummy ticket can be safely deleted after verification without impacting the pipeline or production data.
Input: Create a ticket titled "DUMMY - Automated Testing Setup Verification" with a description indicating it is for pipeline verification.
Expected Output: The ticket is processed by the automated QA pipeline, and the results are logged. The ticket can be deleted after verification.
This is a dummy ticket to verify the automated QA testing pipeline works correctly. Safe to delete after verification.

fields.summary: Timesheets-Block PTO submission on approved Company Holidays for eligible employees

content.text: As an Employee using the Timesheets module,
I want the system to prevent me from submitting a PTO request for a day that is an approved Company Holiday for which I am eligible,
so that I avoid submitting inaccurate or duplicate time-off requests and reduce administrative cleanup for Approvers and HR.
The Timesheets submission logic must check if the requested PTO date falls on a
The system must determine if the submitting employee is
for that specific holiday, based on their employee profile (e.g., employment status or tenure).
the Employee is eligible) are met, the PTO submission must be

A clear, descriptive error message must be displayed to the user when the submission is blocked (e.g., "PTO cannot be submitted for [Holiday Name] as it is an approved Company Holiday for which you are eligible.").
Employee C (Eligible) requests 8 hours of PTO for
*Note on Ineligible: If the employee is ineligible for the paid holiday (e.g., part-time), they may need to request PTO to cover their missed work time, so the block should not apply.

fields.summary: Vendor Admin - Approved Status isn't showing up in Edit in the Status field

content.text: 📝 User Story: Vendor Admin Edit Popup Displays Correct Status
I Want To: See the correct "Approved" status reflected in the status field when editing a vendor that is already approved
So That: I have an accurate and consistent view of the vendor's status across both the grid and the edit popup, reducing confusion and preventing errors.
AC1: When a vendor is set to "Approved" status and appears as approved in the Vendor Admin grid, opening the edit popup for that vendor must display "Approved" in the status field.
AC2: All other statuses (e.g., Pending, Rejected) must continue to display correctly in the edit popup.
AC3: The status field in the edit popup must always match the vendor's current status as shown in the grid.
Input: Vendor "ABC Supplies" is marked as "Approved" in the Vendor Admin grid. User clicks "Edit" for this vendor.
Expected Output: The edit popup opens, and the status field displays "Approved".

In the Vendor Admin, when user opens edit, if the vendor is set to approved status and shows up as approved on the grid, it is not showing approved status in the edit pop up. See screenshot. All other statuses show up correctly.

fields.summary: Project Tracker - Link to Project Cascade Templates in Management Screen

content.text: 📝 User Story: Enhance Project Admin Module with Cascade Template Management
I Want To: Access a direct link to the cascade templates management screen from the project admin module and have a new column in the project list to select a template for each project
So That: I can efficiently manage and assign cascade templates to projects without navigating away from the admin interface.
AC1: The project admin module must include a visible link for each project that navigates directly to that project's cascade templates management screen.
AC2: The project list in the admin module must display a new column allowing the admin to select or change the cascade template for each project.
AC3: Changes to the selected template must be saved and reflected immediately in the project configuration.
AC4: The new features must be accessible to users with project admin permissions only.
Input: Project admin opens the project admin module, sees the new "Cascade Template" column, and clicks the link for "Project A."
Output: Admin is taken to "Project A's" cascade templates management screen. Admin selects a new template from the dropdown in the new column, and the selection is saved and displayed.

in the project admin module give a link to that project cascade templates management screen. then also add a new column to the project list in the project admin module to let them select the template for each project.
*Note: Request came from Carlos in our weekly meeting 4/1/26.

fields.summary: Quoting - Prevent Duplicate POR Approval Notifications for Pre-Approved Requests

content.text: 📝 User Story: Prevent Duplicate POR Approval Notifications for Pre-Approved Requests
I Want To: Not receive approval notifications for PORs that are already pre-approved by the requestor
So That: I am not prompted to take unnecessary duplicate actions, reducing confusion and streamlining the approval process.

AC1: When a POR request is marked as pre-approved by the requestor, the system must automatically approve the POR without sending any approval notification (email or alert bubble) to the designated approver.
AC2: Approvers should only receive notifications for PORs that require their explicit approval.
AC3: No duplicate approval actions should be required for pre-approved PORs.
Input: A POR request (e.g., POR-2660183) is submitted and marked as pre-approved by the requestor.
Expected Output: The system auto-approves the POR. No email or alert bubble is sent to the approver. The approver does not see any pending approval action for this POR.

when the request is listed as pre approved by the requestor, the system auto-approves the POR but for some reason, the system also alerts the approver that they also need to approve that POR, either via an email that comes from SM or via the SM Alert Bubble. You can review POR-2660183 as an example on the production server. This is forcing a duplicate action that is not needed. We need to change that action that when a POR Request is pre-approved, the system does not need to alert the approver.
Request came from Carlos. See attachment.
